title: task.acceptance_criteria is string[] — no AC-addressability for FK depends_on references; no per-criterion status tracking; completion invariant collapses to 'verification field present' rather than schema-level 'every criterion passed'
description: Current .project/schemas/tasks.schema.json declares acceptance_criteria as string[]. Three gaps follow: (1) no addressability — individual criteria have no canonical id, so depends_on (or any other field that might want to reference a specific criterion) cannot FK-target one; (2) no per-criterion status tracking — task status [planned|in-progress|completed|blocked|cancelled] is task-wide; partial completion (criteria 1/2/4 passed but 3/5 pending) is invisible; (3) completion invariant collapse — the only completion check is 'verification field present,' which says nothing about whether each criterion was actually verified. Surfaced immediately by the TASK-080 split (2026-05-26, commits 4bcab3a + da3ec67 + 0a0dcd0): TASK-082 depends on TASK-081; cannot express finer-grained 'TASK-082 depends on TASK-081's criterion #4' (the executeAgent-grant-wired criterion specifically); coarsening forces serial wait on TASK-081's whole completion when partial-completion sequencing would be cheaper. Same FK-as-free-text anti-pattern documented in FGAP-040/041/046 (tasks[].phase, issues[].resolved_by, tasks[].depends_on inline FK arrays).

evidence.reference: acceptance_criteria current shape: string[]; no id pattern, no status field, no completion invariant
TASK-081/082/083/084 just-filed planned tasks (2026-05-26) — TASK-082 depends_on TASK-081 at task-grain only; cannot express criterion-grain depends_on without AC-addressability
verification.criteria_results[].criterion is free-text restating task AC text verbatim (existing duplication this gap's resolution also eliminates via FK linkage)

impact: Affects all tasks under active sequencing where finer-grained criterion-based dependencies would enable parallelism or partial-completion handoff. Affects substrate hygiene: VER entries currently duplicate AC text verbatim instead of FK-referencing. Affects completion invariant strength: 'task is completed' currently means 'verification field is populated' (a presence check) rather than 'every criterion is verified passed' (a content check). Substrate carries weaker completion guarantee than canonical-derivable shape would support.
proposed_resolution: Addressed by FEAT-009 (AC schema revision: object form {id, text, status} with ^TASK-\d{3,}-AC-\d+$ pattern, status enum [pending|passed|failed|blocked], depends_on union(task-id|ac-id), completion invariant shift to 'every criterion.status === passed') per DEC-0051 (open, drafted answer canon-derived). Scoped under .context greenfield transition (not .project in-place migration) — pairs with FEAT-008 as same-arc structural-shape revision. .project tasks continue with string[] form during the transition; object form lands in .context schemas from day one. No data migration required.
